perturbation.original_text: 3. Term. Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the term. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Term. Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter. Either party can terminate with a 60 day written notice. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination only 'after the end of the term', implying the 30-year period. The modified version contradicts this by allowing termination with 60 days' notice 'at any time', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the termination conditions. The contradictory nature of this perturbation creates uncertainty about when the contract can be terminated. The text stating 'unless terminated by a Party upon sixty (60) days' written notice after the end of the term' is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term. Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter. Either party can terminate with a 60 day written notice. Notwithstanding the 30-year term of this agreement, should STW procure sales agreements with customers for a duration of longer than 30 years, STW shall be authorized to maintain and service such contract(s) to the extent of the water volume(s) purchased, and this AGREEMENT shall not terminate during the initial term of any such water supply agreement with a term extending beyond this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated termination could only occur 'after the end of the term', but the changed text allows termination with 60 days' notice 'at any time'. This directly contradicts the initial term, creating an in-text contradiction. This contradicts the section 'Subject to the other provisions contained herein, this AGREEMENT shall be for a term of thirty (30) years from the Effective Date, which shall automatically renew from year-to-year thereafter' because it previously limited the time to terminate to the end of the term, but now allows for any time termination.

perturbation.original_text: 25. Indemnification. The Parties shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend each other, their heirs, successors, and assigns, from and against any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, attorney's fees, expenses, causes of action, suits, claims, and judgments of any kind or character for injury to person or property arising in whole or in part out of the responsibilities set forth in this AGREEMENT, whether due to the negligence of a Party or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: 25. Indemnification. STW shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend COFS, their heirs, successors, and assigns, from and against any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, attorney's fees, expenses, causes of action, suits, claims, and judgments of any kind or character for injury to person or property arising in whole or in part out of the responsibilities set forth in this AGREEMENT, whether due to the negligence of STW or otherwise. COFS is not required to provide indemnification.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that both parties should indemnify, hold harmless, and defend each other. The modified version states that STW is responsible for indemnifying COFS, but COFS does not need to do the same for STW, resulting in a contradiction. This changes the risk allocation within the contract and could lead to disputes. The text stating 'The Parties shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend each other, their heirs, successors, and assigns, from and against any and all losses' is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 25. Indemnification. STW shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend COFS, their heirs, successors, and assigns, from and against any and all losses, liabilities, damages, costs, attorney's fees, expenses, causes of action, suits, claims, and judgments of any kind or character for injury to person or property arising in whole or in part out of the responsibilities set forth in this AGREEMENT, whether due to the negligence of STW or otherwise. COFS is not required to provide indemnification.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'The Parties shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend each other', which means both STW and COFS have this obligation. The changed text explicitly states 'COFS is not required to provide indemnification', directly contradicting the original agreement where both parties were required to indemnify each other. This is a direct and impactful contradiction as it alters the risk allocation and obligations of the parties.
